heading: Prohibitions against smoking on passenger flights
label: 41706 Prohibitions against smoking on passenger flights
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)4170649 App.:1374(d)(1)., , , § 404(d)(1); added , , ; , (less effective date), , 1099.Aug. 23, 1958Dec. 22, 1987Nov. 21, 1989Pub. L. 85–72672 Stat. 731Pub. L. 100–202, § 328(a)101 Stat. 1329–382Pub. L. 101–164, § 335103 Stat. 109849 App.:1374 (note)., (related to effective date), .Nov. 21, 1989Pub. L. 101–164, § 335103 Stat. 1099In subsection (a), before clause (1), the words “On and after the date of expiration of the 4-month period following ” are omitted as executed. The words “of an aircraft” are added for clarity. The text of 49 App.:1374 (note) is omitted as executed.December 22, 1987

Amendments2018—Subsecs. (d), (e). added subsec. (d) and redesignated former subsec. (d) as (e).Pub. L. 115–2542012—, substituted “passenger” for “scheduled” in section catchline.Pub. L. 112–95, § 401(a)(1)Subsecs. (a), (b). , added subsecs. (a) and (b) and struck out former subsecs. (a) and (b) which read as follows:Pub. L. 112–95, § 401(a)(2)“(a) .—An individual may not smoke in an aircraft in scheduled passenger interstate air transportation or scheduled passenger intrastate air transportation.Smoking Prohibition in Intrastate and Interstate Air Transportation“(b) .—The Secretary of Transportation shall require all air carriers and foreign air carriers to prohibit smoking in any aircraft in scheduled passenger foreign air transportation.”Smoking Prohibition in Foreign Air Transportation2000— amended section catchline and text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows:Pub. L. 106–181“(a) .—An individual may not smoke in the passenger cabin or lavatory of an aircraft on a scheduled airline flight segment in air transportation or intrastate air transportation that is—General“(1) between places in a State of the United States, the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, or the Virgin Islands;“(2) between a place in any jurisdiction referred to in clause (1) of this subsection (except Alaska and Hawaii) and a place in any other of those jurisdictions; or“(3)(A) scheduled for not more than 6 hours’ duration; and“(B)(i) between a place referred to in clause (1) of this subsection (except Alaska and Hawaii) and Alaska or Hawaii; or“(ii) between Alaska and Hawaii.“(b) .—The Secretary of Transportation shall prescribe regulations necessary to carry out this section.”Regulations

Effective Date of 2000 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 106–181, title VII, § 708(b)114 Stat. 159Apr. 5, 2000“The amendment made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall take effect on the date that is 60 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”Apr. 5, 2000

children.heading: .—Smoking Prohibition in Interstate and Intrastate Air Transportation
children.label: a .—Smoking Prohibition in Interstate and Intrastate Air Transportation

children.text: in an aircraft in scheduled passenger interstate or intrastate air transportation; or

in an aircraft in nonscheduled passenger interstate or intrastate air transportation, if a flight attendant is a required crewmember on the aircraft (as determined by the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration).

children.heading: .—Smoking Prohibition in Foreign Air Transportation
children.label: b .—Smoking Prohibition in Foreign Air Transportation

children.text: in an aircraft in scheduled passenger foreign air transportation; and
in an aircraft in nonscheduled passenger foreign air transportation, if a flight attendant is a required crewmember on the aircraft (as determined by the Administrator or a foreign government).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: If a foreign government objects to the application of subsection (b) on the basis that subsection (b) provides for an extraterritorial application of the laws of the United States, the Secretary shall waive the application of subsection (b) to a foreign air carrier licensed by that foreign government at such time as an alternative prohibition negotiated under paragraph (2) becomes effective and is enforced by the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Alternative prohibition
children.label: 2 .—Alternative prohibition
children.text: If, pursuant to paragraph (1), a foreign government objects to the prohibition under subsection (b), the Secretary shall enter into bilateral negotiations with the objecting foreign government to provide for an alternative smoking prohibition.

children.heading: .—Inclusion
children.label: 1 .—Inclusion
children.text: The use of an electronic cigarette shall be treated as smoking for purposes of this section.

children.heading: .—Electronic cigarette defined
children.label: 2 .—Electronic cigarette defined
children.text: In this section, the term “electronic cigarette” means a device that delivers nicotine to a user of the device in the form of a vapor that is inhaled to simulate the experience of smoking.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: e .—Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as are necessary to carry out this section.
